verdict_reasoning: TMED9 is a type I transmembrane protein whose large N-terminal domain faces the ER/Golgi lumen and whose short C-terminal tail is cytosolic, binding COPI/COPII coats. Its dominant steady-state localization is the ER, ERGIC, and cis-Golgi, cycling within the early secretory pathway. The luminal domain is never exposed to the extracellular space. No surface-directed therapeutic program, surface biotinylation data, or flow-cytometry evidence for outer-leaflet TMED9 display was found. Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented cancer/cell-death/activation ecto-pool for TMED9 itself; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no narrow developmental/germline outer-leaflet display reported; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — TMED9 is not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — PM references in the literature describe cargo (misfolded GPI-APs) transiting through the PM, not TMED9 residing there; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to the outer leaflet documented. The protein is an endomembrane-resident trafficking factor with no outer-leaflet biology.
